name: IndexTTS
tagline: Zero-shot text-to-speech with voice cloning and independent emotion control
metaDescription: IndexTTS2 is an open autoregressive text-to-speech model with zero-shot voice cloning, emotion control, and precise duration control. Install and run in Python.
overview: IndexTTS2 is a text-to-speech model from bilibili's Index team. You give it a short reference clip of a voice plus some text, and it speaks that text in the cloned voice without any per-speaker training (zero-shot). It runs as a Python package and ships its weights on Hugging Face and ModelScope.

The model is built for developers who need expressive, controllable speech. It separates speaker identity from emotion, so you can keep one person's timbre while changing the emotional tone from a separate style prompt. It also supports a duration-control mode that can fix the number of generated tokens, which helps when speech has to line up with video.

Within the speech and audio space, IndexTTS2 sits alongside other autoregressive zero-shot TTS systems. It targets cases where word accuracy, speaker similarity, and emotional fidelity all matter, such as dubbing and voiceover work.
features: Zero-shot voice cloning from a single short reference audio clip
Independent control of timbre and emotion, using separate speaker and style prompts
Two duration modes: precise token-count control, or free autoregressive generation that follows the prompt's prosody
Soft emotion control through natural-language text descriptions
Pre-trained weights distributed on Hugging Face and ModelScope
Python inference API (indextts.infer_v2.IndexTTS2) with optional FP16, CUDA kernel, and DeepSpeed flags

gettingStarted.intro: IndexTTS2 is a Python project. You clone the repo, install dependencies with uv, download the model weights, then call the inference API. Make sure git and git-lfs are installed first.

steps.heading: Clone the repository
steps.body: Clone the repo and pull the large files tracked by git-lfs.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/index-tts/index-tts.git && cd index-tts
git lfs pull

steps.heading: Install dependencies
steps.body: The project uses uv to manage its environment. Install all extras.

steps.heading: Download the model weights
steps.body: Pull the IndexTTS-2 checkpoints from Hugging Face into a local checkpoints folder.
steps.code:
uv tool install "huggingface-hub[cli,hf_xet]"
hf download IndexTeam/IndexTTS-2 --local-dir=checkpoints

steps.heading: Synthesize speech in Python
steps.body: Load the model from the checkpoints directory and pass a reference voice clip plus your text. The cloned voice is read from spk_audio_prompt and the result is written to output_path.
steps.code:
from indextts.infer_v2 import IndexTTS2

tts = IndexTTS2(
    cfg_path="checkpoints/config.yaml",
    model_dir="checkpoints",
    use_fp16=False,
    use_cuda_kernel=False,
    use_deepspeed=False,
)

text = "Translate for me, what is a surprise!"
tts.infer(
    spk_audio_prompt='examples/voice_01.wav',
    text=text,
    output_path="gen.wav",
    verbose=True,
)

useCases: Dub video or animation, using duration control to keep generated speech aligned with the original timing
Clone a single speaker's voice from a short sample to narrate articles, audiobooks, or product walkthroughs
Generate the same line in different emotional tones by swapping the style prompt while keeping one voice
Prototype voice assistants or characters that need consistent timbre with adjustable emotional delivery
